description: Estimate the intercept and slope of a simple linear regression of Y on X using ordinary least squares — minimising the sum of squared vertical residuals

fields.name: x_observations
fields.label: Array of independent variable values (same length as y)

ols_estimators.slope: b1_hat = sum_i [(X_i - X_bar)(Y_i - Y_bar)] / sum_i [(X_i - X_bar)^2] = Cov(X,Y) / Var(X)
ols_estimators.regression_line_passes_through_means: The fitted line always passes through the point (X_bar, Y_bar)

interpretation.slope: b1_hat estimates the average change in Y for a one-unit change in X
interpretation.intercept: b0_hat estimates the expected Y when X = 0 (may be economically meaningless if X = 0 is outside the sample)
interpretation.residual_sum_zero: Sum of OLS residuals equals zero when an intercept is included

applications.factor_model: Regress stock excess return on market excess return to estimate alpha (b0) and beta (b1)
applications.growth_relationship: Regress market value on earnings or cash flow
applications.cost_of_equity: Slope of return vs market = CAPM beta

validation.equal_length: x_observations and y_observations must have the same length
validation.sample_size: n >= 2 required to fit a line; n >= 10 preferred for inference
validation.x_variation_positive: Var(X) > 0 — constant X cannot explain variation in Y

length_mismatch.description: x and y arrays differ in length

errors.message: Independent variable has zero variance; regression cannot be estimated
X and Y arrays must have the same length

anti_patterns.rule: Do not interpret the intercept when X = 0 is outside the observed range
anti_patterns.why: Extrapolation outside the sample support is unreliable; the intercept is an arithmetic byproduct, not a forecast of Y at X = 0

anti_patterns.rule: Do not swap the roles of dependent and independent variables casually
anti_patterns.why: OLS minimises vertical residuals. Regressing Y on X and X on Y produce different slopes — choose based on which variable you wish to predict

anti_patterns.rule: Do not fit OLS to data with a clear non-linear pattern
anti_patterns.why: Linear regression assumes E[Y|X] is linear in X; ignoring curvature produces biased forecasts. Transform variables or use a non-linear model

anti_patterns.rule: Do not report coefficients without units and sample size
anti_patterns.why: b1 = 0.5 is meaningless without knowing Y and X scales, and without n the uncertainty is hidden

goals.description: Estimate the intercept and slope of a simple linear regression of Y on X using ordinary least squares — minimising the sum of squared vertical residuals

success_metrics.measurement: All decisions have complete audit trails

constraints.description: All operations must be auditable and traceable

tradeoffs.reason: trading operations require precise execution and full audit trails

invariants: error messages never expose internal system details
